note: ENHANCED PR Freelance pipeline scrape. 7-day window across LinkedIn (Google-indexed), Indeed, ZipRecruiter, startup.jobs, web3.career, Mediabistro, and aggregator boards. Deduped against pr-freelance-scrape reports from 2026-04-30 through 2026-05-15. Mix of explicit freelance/contract roles (Lemonade, Carwow, Liftoff) and full-time openings worth pitching freelance/interim coverage on while client hires (Tether, BitGo, Ethos). Lemonade is the strongest fit: a six-month NYC-or-remote contract anchored in agency management and earned media — exactly Mark's wheelhouse.

leads.name: Lemonade

fitDetails.pr_remit: Senior Comms Manager, contract — explicit earned media + corporate comms + agency management
fitDetails.sector_fit: Insurance/fintech — directly in Mark's sweet spot
fitDetails.workstream: Earned media strategy, narrative crafting, agency partner management, comms ops scaling
fitDetails.buyer_quality: Public insurtech in 'critical growth moment' — well-funded, senior in-house team
fitDetails.urgency: 6-month defined contract with weekly comp range published — they need someone now

intentDetails.recency: Posted ~1 month ago; still active across LinkedIn/Lemonade careers page
intentDetails.urgency_signals: Defined term + agency-management remit + 'fast-moving comms function' language
intentDetails.apply_path: Direct apply via makers.lemonade.com careers page

contacts.name: Corporate Communications hiring team
contacts.title: Director of Communications (hiring manager — name not public)

signals: Agency management = ideal for senior freelancer who has run agencies
leads.source: Lemonade Careers (cross-posted LinkedIn, RevOps Careers, ZipHire)
leads.notes: Highest-priority lead this week. Mark should apply directly AND reach out on LinkedIn to current Lemonade comms leadership offering coverage. Even if they hire someone, the agency-management language suggests room for a parallel retainer.

leads.name: Tether

fitDetails.pr_remit: PR Manager role explicitly stated — media relations, narrative, crisis-adjacent given regulatory pressure
fitDetails.sector_fit: Crypto/fintech — Mark's financial-comms background translates directly
fitDetails.workstream: Media relations, executive comms, reputation/crisis management
fitDetails.buyer_quality: Largest stablecoin issuer; high-profile, scrutiny-heavy brand

intentDetails.engagement_model: Full-time per posting, BUT high candidate-search burden + reputational stakes = strong fit for interim/fractional bridge
intentDetails.recency: Active in May 2026; appears on web3.career, cryptocurrencyjobs.co, Tether's own board
intentDetails.urgency_signals: Crypto firms historically take months to fill senior PR — interim coverage is easy pitch

signals: Crypto/stablecoin — Mark's fintech background is rare in this niche
High-pressure reputational environment = need for senior interim coverage
leads.notes: Pitch angle: 'Senior interim PR/comms coverage while you complete your search.' Even if Tether locks in a full-timer, the role's scope (global media, regulators, executive narrative) virtually guarantees they'll need agency/contractor support afterward.

leads.name: Ethos Technologies

fitDetails.pr_remit: Post-IPO comms need is structural: earnings narrative, analyst-day prep, investor media, executive thought leadership
fitDetails.sector_fit: Life-insurance fintech — Sequoia/Accel/SoftBank backed, exactly Mark's ICP
fitDetails.workstream: Investor narrative, executive comms, thought leadership, earned media for product launches
fitDetails.buyer_quality: Newly public $1.1B-valuation fintech with growth thesis to defend each quarter
fitDetails.urgency: First post-IPO earnings cycle — comms ramp-up is happening now

intentDetails.engagement_model: No public job posting — outbound pitch lead for retainer/project work
intentDetails.remote_flex: HQ Palo Alto but post-IPO comms often runs distributed
intentDetails.recency: IPO closed Jan 2026; comms infrastructure still maturing
intentDetails.urgency_signals: Quarterly earnings cadence creates predictable retainer windows
intentDetails.apply_path: Direct outreach to Head of Comms / IR via LinkedIn

contacts.name: Ethos Comms / IR team
contacts.title: Head of Comms / Head of IR (identify via LinkedIn)

signals: Survived insurtech consolidation — strong narrative to build on
Quarterly reporting cadence creates ongoing retainer opportunity
leads.source: S-1 + post-IPO coverage (IPOScoop, S&P Global, TechBuzz)
leads.notes: Outbound lead, not a posted role. Mark should map the comms/IR org via LinkedIn and offer specialized post-IPO retainer support: media training for new public-company execs, earnings press strategy, analyst-day prep. Many newly public companies in this size band still rely on outside counsel for ~12 months.

leads.name: carwow

fitDetails.pr_remit: PR & Communications Manager role explicitly framed as Freelance/Contract
fitDetails.sector_fit: Not core (auto/marketplace), but consumer brand with strong B2B side; transferable
fitDetails.workstream: Media relations, brand storytelling, product launch comms
fitDetails.buyer_quality: Established VC-backed scaleup, real PR function
fitDetails.urgency: Posting live; freelance framing means they want someone fast

intentDetails.recency: Recent — actively cross-posted on startup.jobs, Wellfound, Workable
intentDetails.urgency_signals: Freelance framing on a typically full-time role suggests gap-fill

signals: Cross-posted across multiple boards — active hiring
Established VC-backed company with real PR ops
Location skew (UK) is a constraint — pitch as US/international media lead or remote project
leads.notes: Lower priority due to UK location, but rare to see an explicit freelance PR Manager posting. If Mark is open to US-based remote project work for European brands, this is a viable angle. Otherwise treat as market signal.

leads.name: Liftoff

fitDetails.pr_remit: Internal Communications Manager — primarily internal, NOT external media
fitDetails.sector_fit: Ad-tech / B2B SaaS — adjacent to Mark's ICP but not core financial
fitDetails.workstream: Internal comms (emails, Slack, intranet, exec talking points) — narrower than Mark's typical scope
fitDetails.buyer_quality: PE-backed (Blackstone) SaaS scaleup, mature comms function
fitDetails.urgency: Hourly contract — they want someone billable now

intentDetails.remote_flex: Remote, preferred Pacific Time but US-wide eligibility
intentDetails.urgency_signals: Hourly billing structure + 4+ yrs requirement = procurement-ready
intentDetails.apply_path: Apply via Built In / RemoteRocketship / company careers

contacts.name: Director of Communications, Employee Experience & Engagement
contacts.title: Hiring Manager

signals: Internal comms only (narrower than Mark's typical media-relations work)
leads.notes: Lower fit because it's internal comms, but the engagement structure (hourly contract, remote, PE-backed) is exactly the procurement pattern Mark is set up for. Worth applying if Mark wants steady billable hours; pitch his media-relations chops as a bonus.

leads.name: BitGo

fitDetails.sector_fit: Crypto/fintech infrastructure — strong fit for financial-comms operator
fitDetails.workstream: Media relations, executive comms, regulatory narrative
fitDetails.buyer_quality: Major institutional crypto custodian, regulated entity

intentDetails.urgency_signals: Crypto-comms talent scarce — interim coverage compelling

signals: Crypto custody / institutional crypto — Mark's financial-comms angle is rare in this niche
Regulated entity — needs PR ops that understands compliance language
SF location is a constraint for purely remote engagement
leads.notes: Lower intent score because it's posted as full-time perm and SF-anchored. Best play is outbound: 'I can plug in as interim Head of Comms while you complete your search.' Realistic close probability is lower than Lemonade but worth a single touchpoint.
